title: Embedded-sprint materialization and eager wiring into plan
description: Extend materializeContracts to prefer valid embedded spec.sprints: safeParse each entry against SprintContractSchema, normalize status to proposed, and on any parse or precision failure fall back to feature-derived generation without throwing. Wire runPlanCommand to call materializeContracts after a successful non-clarification plan, clearing the current specId's prior contracts first, and correct the next-step hint so it points to a command that now functions.

successCriteria.description: Running npm run build and the typecheck completes with zero errors after wiring eager materialization into the plan command.

Given a spec whose sprints array entries each satisfy SprintContractSchema, materializeContracts writes one contract file per entry with status normalized to proposed and the embedded success criteria and precision fields preserved.

Given a spec whose sprints entries omit status or use an unknown verificationMethod, materializeContracts does not throw and instead writes feature-derived contracts that satisfy SprintContractSchema.
After runPlanCommand completes for a ready spec the contracts directory contains schema-valid files, while a spec with status needs-clarification yields zero contract files.
Re-running materialization for the same specId overwrites that specId's prior contracts and leaves no stale higher-numbered files behind.
The plan command prints a next-step hint that points to a command which succeeds, consistent with the hint printed by plan answer.

nonGoals: Do not add a separate contracts CLI command.
Do not modify the sprint command's contract selection in this sprint; that is Sprint 3.
stopConditions: Stop when plan writes valid contracts from embedded sprints with feature-derived fallback and the needs-clarification path writes none.
Stop when re-planning the same spec does not accumulate stale contract files.
definitionOfDone: The plan command eagerly materializes schema-valid sprint contracts from embedded spec.sprints when present, falls back to feature-derived generation otherwise, skips materialization for needs-clarification specs, avoids stale-file accumulation, and prints an accurate next-step hint.
assumptions: Embedded entries that fail safeParse trigger a whole-set feature-derived fallback rather than a partial mix, keeping a single id scheme per spec.
Clearing prior contracts is scoped to files whose specId matches the current spec, leaving other specs untouched.
outOfScope: Honoring embedded sprints inside run is already delivered via the shared helper from Sprint 1 and needs no extra plan-side work.
generatorNotes: Add the embedded branch to materializeContracts from Sprint 1: iterate spec.sprints, SprintContractSchema.safeParse each, override id with deterministic sprint-<specId>-NN, force status to proposed, and pass through saveContract (which still enforces the precision gate). If any entry fails to parse, log and fall back to the feature-derived branch for the whole spec. In runPlanCommand (plan.ts), after printPlan and only when result.kind is not needs-clarification, call materializeContracts(spec, projectRoot, config). Before writing, delete existing .bober/contracts files whose parsed specId equals spec.specId. Update printPlan's final hint; reconcile the inconsistency where plan suggests sprint (plan.ts:171) but plan answer suggests run (plan.ts:290).
evaluatorNotes: Test three paths: valid embedded sprints used verbatim with normalized status; malformed embedded sprints falling back to feature-derived without throwing; needs-clarification spec producing zero contracts. Verify idempotency by materializing a 3-sprint spec, then a 2-sprint version of the same specId, and asserting only two files remain. Confirm the printed hint names a command that the new code makes functional.
